summary: Tag each Agricola card with the mechanics/features it uses

cards.note: Continuous +1 capacity on a clay/stone room adjacent to both a field and a pasture (orthogonal adjacency/shape); can be lost -> derived, not latched.
Places stables on rounds+3/6/9 round spaces; at start of those rounds you may build a stable free (granted sub-action, stable piece from supply).
Each time you use a wood accumulation space, optionally pay 3 food to plow 1 field; food provenance restricted (not after-space food).
After adding rooms, optionally pay 1 wood+1 grain for a Family-Growth-with-room-only action; exactly once regardless of rooms (per-add, but limited).
Declining an unconditional Sow lets you place another person on any space even if occupied (extra placement, occupied-space legal).
After using Fishing while <=2 food there, get a Major-or-Minor Improvement action (conditional on the named space's good count).
On play, and each time a stone accumulation space is revealed on a round space in prep, get 1 wood + a Minor Improvement action.
Each time you get a Bake Bread action, instead play an occupation for free; limited once per turn.
Scoring: +2 bonus points per pasture covering >=3 farmyard spaces (plain per-pasture threshold count).
In returning-home phase, if you return >=3 people from accumulation spaces, buy 1 bonus point for 1 food; needs to count returned-from-accumulation workers.
Scoring: +1 bonus point per cooking improvement (ovens excluded); plain count.
Taking 1/2/3+ food from a food accumulation space also grants veg/grain/reed by tier; needs amount taken from that space.
In each harvest's field phase, get wood/food per wood accumulation space by its wood count; references specific board spaces' goods.
For each new field tile you get (plow), also get 1 clay + 1 food.
Fires in field phase when the last crop is taken from a field, giving food; keyed on field-harvest depletion event.
On placing your Nth person of the round on a resource space with exact count, get food; needs count of placements this round and space contents.
Each time you take Major-or-Minor action to play an improvement, also get 3 food.
Each time you use Lessons, place a veg on qualifying fields; adds crops onto fields (field manipulation).
Each time you use a wood accumulation space, get an optional Sow sub-action for one field.
On play, get 1 wood per improvement in front of you; plain count-scaled goods.
Field phase each harvest: auto get 1 wood, plus optionally buy up to 2 wood for 1 food each (convert food to wood, capped per harvest).
On play get 1 clay, and each time after using Lessons or Clay Pit get 1 clay.
Each time you use Grain Utilization, also get 2 clay and 1 wood.
Before using an action space card on round spaces 5-7, get 1 stone; depends on specific round-space numbers.
Before using an action space card on round spaces 1/2/3/4, get the corresponding resource; keyed on specific round-space numbers.
Provides a room-slot for one person until round 9 returning-home, then removed; a temporary person-holder needing a deadline flag. Not a room.
Each time another player uses Reed Bank, you may build a room with a clay/stone discount; opponent-triggered granted build with cost modification (room holds a person).
After using Farm Expansion or Grain Seeds, jump the same person to the other space (even if occupied), once per turn; moves an already-placed worker to a second/occupied space.
Once per round you may place a later person on the space holding your 2nd person and reuse it — uses an occupied space, needs to know which space the 2nd person is on.
After building Joinery/Pottery/Basketmaker via any means, may play up to 2 occupations free — hooks the build-major sub-action, grants a play-occupation action.
Before another player sows, may give them 1 grain to earn 1 bonus point — opponent sub-action hook, goods transfer, bonus-point rule.
On play +1 wood +1 clay; scoring bonus per pasture holding max animals (a per-pasture threshold count, not an optimization).
On-play wood scaled by rounds left; scoring bonus for each player meeting animal thresholds — comparative (all players).
On-play wood scaled by rounds left; scoring bonus for each player meeting building-resource thresholds — comparative.
Each time you use Sheep Market or Reed Bank, may also take 1 good from the other named accumulation space.
On play grants a Major-Improvement action to build Fireplace (its cost); plus a hook: each time you use Hollow, get 1 food.
Each use of Clay Pit/Hollow gives food equal to clay currently on the other space — must read the amount on the other space.
At start of returning-home each round, if both named spaces are occupied, +1 veg — round-end hook conditioned on board occupancy.
Each time any player takes >=2 reed from Reed Bank, choose 1 grain or buy 1 veg for 2 food — fires on own and opponent action.
Place 3 reed+4 clay on card; next player to reach 3 pastures / 5 fields gets them — standing-condition latch delivering a stashed pile.
At end of each harvest, get 1 reed — harvest-end hook (implicitly once per harvest).
Taking exactly 2/3/4/5 food from a food accumulation space also grants stone/reed/clay/wood — needs the amount taken.
Card is a holder for sheep with capacity = number of completed feeding phases; a running count drives its slot capacity.
Each use of Traveling Players grants an optional Build Rooms action with rooms costing 1 reed less.
When another player uses Traveling Players, you may take a Build Fences/Stables/Rooms action of your choice.
At start of returning-home, if exactly one of round-spaces 1-4 is unoccupied you may use that space's effect without placing; depends on board positions and unrevealed-space state.
In each returning-home phase where no one returns from Lessons, you may play an occupation for 1 food; conditional on the Lessons space usage.
First time you have 5 wild boars after playing, get 3 bonus points once; fires at a count threshold.
When another uses a food accumulation space, you may give them 1 grain to gain 1 bonus point; scoring via a fired transfer, tag E-SCORE for the bonus point mechanic.
When another uses reed/stone/sheep/boar accumulation space, pay them 1 food to get 1 matching good from supply.
At start of returning-home, if a Lessons space is unoccupied get 1 food; conditional read of space occupancy.
When another uses Traveling Players, you may convert your choice of grain/sheep/veg to 4/5/7 food.
When another uses Fishing/Reed Bank, give them 1 wood to get 2/3 food from general supply.
Using a second accumulation space of the same good-type in a work phase gives +1 veg; needs to track which good-types already collected this phase.
When both Forest and Clay Pit are occupied, you may use the gap between them as a space for 2 clay + 3 wood; depends on board adjacency/occupancy, no trigger event so at-will.
Using Resource Market with 1st/2nd/.../5th person placed gives a corresponding bonus good; needs to know how many people placed / which placement.
Each time you take wood from a wood accumulation space, may exchange 1 wood for 1 sheep (wood returned to space); animal must be accommodated.
Immediately before each harvest, may buy 1 cattle for food (reduced per pasture, min 0); food payable by conversion; animal accommodated.
Building >=1 room AND >=1 stable same turn also grants an animal; fires on the build sub-actions; mandatory gain; needs accommodation.
After using a Lessons space, optionally buy an animal for food; food may need conversion; animal accommodated.

On play get 1 wood OR 1 grain (mandatory choice); passive: keep any 1 animal per room of house (raises house-pet capacity per room). E-MANDCHOICE is not a code; correcting to F-MANDCHOICE not applicable (no hook).
May renovate wood->stone directly (skip clay), declinable; extends renovation target eligibility, changing what/how renovation is done.
Each Build Fences action, 3 fences free of wood; only the literal Build Fences action per errata.

Gated on living in stone house (standing condition -> ST-LATCH enables), then at start of each round may pay 1 food to plow. The stone-house gate is an eligibility latch, but the recurring grant is a start-of-round HOOK granting a plow sub-action for food. Unclear whether to model gate as LATCH vs prerequisite-condition.

For 1 food, take an action with an offspring the same round it's gained and it doesn't count as newborn; hooks off family growth, changes newborn classification. Timing/optionality complex; food payable by conversion.
End-game +1 per unfenced stable; plain count score computed at scoring.
On play pile goods on card; at any time buy top good for 1 food (LIFO stack consumed over time).
After using a wood accumulation space, may exchange 1 wood for 2 food (wood returned to space).
Building >=1 clay room or renovating clay->stone also grants 3 food; mandatory; fires on build/renovate sub-action.
Each time you build >=1 room, may place 1 food on each of next 4 round spaces, collected at those rounds' start.
On-play +1 grain; changes field-phase yield per grain field (extra grain from each) = E-CROPMANIP, fires in harvest field phase automatically.
Each use of Day Laborer -> +1 grain (auto), with a from-round-6 grain-or-veg choice; net a gain hook on a named space.
Each use of a wood accumulation space -> +1 wood, mandatory.
Using any of 4 named spaces -> at end of that turn +1 wood; clarification confirms per-turn firing, at-end-of-turn = T-AFTER.
First moment you no longer live in wood house -> place 2 clay on next 5 round spaces (future-round scheduling).
On build-room/renovate, once per room/action, replace 2 clay/stone with 1 wood = optional cost substitution.
On play, conditional plain goods (clay house exactly 2 rooms) -> gain clay/reed/stone; plain state read.
End-game bonus-point rule scaling with improvement count; plain threshold score.
Each Fishing use: optionally pay 1 wood to get food-per-person + 1 reed; optional gain hook. Cost is wood (resource), gains food; per-person food read.
Every improvement/room/renovation costs 1 stone less = always-on cost reduction.
Each animal-market use: optionally buy 1 extra animal for 1 food; provides animals to accommodate, food cost.
Each Traveling Players use -> +1 wood +1 grain, mandatory.
When ANOTHER player uses Traveling Players: +1 food +1 wood (auto), then optional buy 1 veg for 2 food (convert). Opp-action hook.
On play +1 boar (animal). Breeds at end of round 12 (outside normal breeding phase / single-animal breed) = E-BREEDMOD, conditional on room.
Each use of Day Laborer grants an optional build-1-room-or-renovate sub-action, cost still paid; extends where these subactions are available.
On play +1 wood; once in stone house (latch), each round start optional build 1 stable for 1 wood (stable = piece).
Each use of Day Laborer grants an optional plow-1-field sub-action.
Each major improvement build: reduce stone cost by rooms built onto initial house (a computed count snapshot).
Once in stone house (latch), each round start optionally play an occupation (1 food) or a minor improvement (its cost).
Scoring bonus: 1 pt per pasture with >=1 animal and unused capacity for 3+ more; plain per-pasture count, not an optimization.
Scoring bonus: 1 pt per occupation played after this one; needs ordering count.
On play, immediate goods (2p: 3 clay); in some player counts sheep, which must be accommodated.
At any time exchange 1 farmyard sheep for 1 wild boar / 1 veg / 1 stone; boar option provides an animal.
Once in stone house (first-becomes-true), place 3 food on each remaining round space, collected at those round starts.
Each use of a wood accumulation space grants an additional (optional) Bake Bread sub-action; named-space class, not board geometry.
Before playing each later occupation, may pay 1 wood to get 1 food per occupation in front of you; counts occupations.
Each round start, if >=3 rooms & only 2 people, get 1 food and 1 crop of choice (grain/veg = mandatory choice).
While house has exactly 2 rooms, each round start get 1 wood; condition re-checked each round so a hook, not a latch.
Each time you use Forest/Reed Bank space, also get 1 clay (before, auto).
On play, pay 1 food to get 1 stone per room; food cost payable.
Rooms cost 3 building resource + 2 reed — passive cost change.
On play gain wood by rounds-left; scoring: most-rooms comparative bonus.
Each use of Grain Seeds also get 1 vegetable.
On renovate/build-room, may replace reed with 1 wood — optional cost substitution.
Each use of Resource Market: get 1 clay or 1 grain (choice, no decline).
Once you are sole player in 2-room house, gain resources once — standing condition latch.
Place 1 sheep on rounds +2/5/8/10; collected at start; must accommodate.
Each use of Grain Seeds, may buy 1 cattle for 1 food — optional buy.
With 2x2 fields, a Build Stables action may build a center stable holding a person; adjacency/shape; once only.
Card holds wild boar = number of completed feeding phases; new holder, running count.
On play: free renovation (no resources) then may take Build Fences action; renovate cost overridden to free.
Using Farmland while Grain Seeds occupied, may plow 1 extra field; depends on another space's occupancy state.
Each time you use sheep Market (from R5), optional Family Growth with room only sub-action -> hook on space, T-BEFORE default, granted growth (optional). Round-5 gate is a computable condition.
Place food on future round spaces (E-SCHED); at start of those rounds get food back + choose sow or Build Fences (mandatory choose between two actions).
Before Fencing space: +2 wood (auto). After that action: optionally place another person -> extra placement.
Place wood on next 2 odd-numbered round spaces (odd-numbered -> board position dependency); at start get wood + a Minor Improvement action.
End-game bonus: 1 pt per later-played own card with 'accumulation space(s)' text -> scoring rule, count of qualifying cards.
Each harvest, if any player owns Joinery/upgrade, optionally buy 1 bonus point for 2 food. Buying VP with food each harvest.
On play +1 wood +1 reed. Each time you decline a Minor/Major Improvement action, get 1 food/veg instead -> hook on the improvement action's decline.
On play, gain 1 of each good type where you already have >=2 of it. Conditional plain-state read of immediate goods -> NONE-like.
Immediately before start of each round, if goods promised to you remain on a round space, +1 food. Reads scheduled/promised goods.
End of each preparation phase (after round reveal), if >=1 stone: +1 food (+1 veg in R14). Fires at prep-phase end -> nearest seam is the reveal/prep boundary.
For each clay room you build: +2 food +1 bonus point. Hook on build-room sub-action (clay only); also a per-clay-room end score.
Each time you use the action space card revealed right before the most recent harvest (R4/7/9/11/13): +1 grain. Board-order dependency.
On play, optionally buy grain (1 food) or veg (3 food) -> mandatory-ish choice conversion paid in food. Card is a field (sow/harvest on it).
Passive +1 animal per stable-less pasture (capacity growth). Each time you sow, optionally add 1 extra crop of the sown type in that field -> crop manipulation on the field.
On play +1 wood; plus fires each time reed is placed on empty Reed Bank in preparation phase (a refill/reveal event) -> +1 wood.
On play +1 wood; after each work phase, conditional state read (stone>clay) -> +1 wood.
Immediate +1 wood; places wood on next round spaces up to fences built, collected at round start.
Before using the space left of the most-recently-placed round-space card -> +2 clay; depends on board position/order.
Each time you use a clay/stone accumulation space, also get the other good.
After enclosing >=1 new farmyard space (fencing sub-action, not subdivision) -> +2 stone.
Places wood/clay/reed/stone on next 4 round spaces, collected at round start.
On play (round 5+), optionally pay stone/grain/veg/sheep for Family Growth even without room. Costs are normal; sheep is spent not gained. Round-5 restriction is a computable prereq.
After using Major Improvement space, optionally take a renovation action paying 2 clay/stone less.
Use round-space-13 action even if occupied by adjacent players; specific round-space number.
After using Grain Utilization/Fencing while the other is unoccupied, pay 1 food to use the other with the same person; once per turn; worker ends on second space.
After using a wood accumulation space, optionally play a minor improvement (not the Minor Improvement action).

Latches once farmyard is full, then a bonus point per vegetable harvested going forward. Ambiguity: bonus accrues per-harvest event (a running counter fed by a hook), not a plain end-game count — straddles hook-counter and E-SCORE.
At start of scoring, gain vegetables = min(majors, minors, occupations). Gains goods at the pre-scoring seam, not a bonus-point rule.
Fires after a renovate-to-stone if done by a given round; grants food + bonus points once. Round threshold snapshot needs store; scoring rule is a text bonus.
Start-of-round optional exchange of animal+grain+veg for 5 food and 2 bonus points.
Place goods on card; each use of a round-space card on spaces 8-11 draws corresponding good. Goods stack on card; keyed to specific round-space numbers.
On play +2 wood; each time another player takes >=5 wood they pay you 1 food. Needs the amount taken (provenance).
In returning-home phase, if no wood left on board, gain 1 food (2 from round 5). Condition is a plain board read.
End of each work phase where you placed >=1 good on a farmyard space, get 2 food; moving an existing good does not count -> needs provenance of the placement.
On play optional tiered clay->grain exchange; the card itself is a field (sow/harvest on it).
Each time another player uses Hollow, you get 1 clay (extra in 3/4p). Named space, opponent action.
After you use Clay Pit or Hollow, get 1 wood (+1 reed on Clay Pit). Named spaces, own action, after.
Each time you use a building-resource accumulation space, optionally discard a hand card for 1 more of the accumulating type. Needs which resource type accumulated; discards a hand card.
After using a wood accumulation space, optionally pay 1 grain for 1 wild boar (animal must be accommodated).
On play +1 sheep; each occupation in front of you can hold 1 sheep (new holders).
On play, remove 3 stables from supply to build a 2-space pasture paying only 2 wood total for fences. Reduced fence cost, geometry of pasture, stables consumed as pieces.

After using Farm Expansion or Major Improvement while the other is unoccupied, pay 1 food to use that other space with the same person; once per turn. Person moves to second space; pairing of the two specific spaces.
On-play +1 stone; while in wood house with exactly 2 rooms, spaces (except Meeting Place) treated as unoccupied — continuous L-OCCUPY.
After using Day Laborer, may pay 1 food to use unoccupied Traveling Players/Lessons with same person; granting another space's action.
End-game bonus-point rule totaling major-improvement values with smallest doubled; thresholds give VP. Plain E-SCORE.
Once this game, first time you have 7 sheep: +3 VP + 2 food — one-shot count-threshold latch.
On-play +1 wood +1 reed; each time paying occupation cost, may draw food from Traveling Players accumulation space — changes cost currency source.
At any time, pay 1 animal to place food on next N round spaces collected at start; ATWILL, schedules food onto future rounds.
End of each work phase, if you used both Forest and Grove, get 5 food; needs to know which spaces you placed on.
Each time ANOTHER player places a field adjacent to an existing field, you get 1 food (grain in r14); opponent action, adjacency-dependent.
On play + end of each work phase where Forest/Clay Pit/Reed Bank all occupied, get 1 grain; reads board occupancy at phase end.
Each of your turns, if a 5+-goods space exists and you don't use any such space, get 1 veg; per-turn conditional on placement choice.
Each time you obtain exactly 2/3/4 wood from a wood accumulation space, +1 wood and food; clarification: fires before leave-on-space decision.
Immediately before each harvest, may discard grain from different fields to get wild boars; harvest-start hook, crop-on-field cost, provides animals.
On play, may pay 2 food to get 1 of each animal, only if all accommodatable; optional on-play animals with food cost.
Each time you renovate, +2 food and +1 animal of respective type in each pasture with a stable; renovate sub-action hook granting goods+animals.
On play gain 1 grain; each grain becomes a continuous new holder for 1 animal of any type.
Stores a stone room on the card; once condition met, at any time add it for free (grants a build-room sub-action at no resource cost).
Each time you use Grain Seeds space, optionally also plow 1 field (before, granted sub-action).
Card is a personal action space; nth use gives a begging marker plus escalating count of different goods of choice; needs use-counter.
On play +3 food; +1 food per vegetable harvested from fields during the field phase.
On play and at start of each feeding phase you may take a Bake Bread action.

cards.new_category: SKIP-HARVEST: on play, skip (both field/feed/breed and feeding obligation of) the next harvest entirely.
cards.note: Fits no code; must mark next harvest to be skipped including no feeding requirement.
During each feeding phase, optionally convert exactly 1 vegetable into 5 food.

After field phase, optionally turn 1 grain into a chosen 3 food OR 1 bonus point; the option itself is optional (F-TRIG) but reward is a choice; note bonus-point route. Actually optional use then mandatory choice.
Before using Grain Utilization/Farmland/Cultivation, optionally buy 1 grain for 1 food; must fire before other before-effects.
On play +1 grain; at end of each harvest optionally buy 1 vegetable for 2 food.
Place 1 wood on each of next 5 round spaces, collected at start of those rounds.
On play +1 wood +1 clay; after each renovate, gain wood equal to number of people placed that round; needs count of workers placed.
On play +1 wood +2 clay; after using Grain Seeds or Vegetable Seeds space, +1 clay.
Always-on cost reduction on improvements/renovations/rooms.
At start of each work phase gain clay/stone while standing house/room condition holds; recurring conditional gain, not a one-time latch.
After using Day Laborer: +wood/clay and optional buy stone for food (conversion).
Immediate wood by rounds-left (plain state read); bonus points depend on each player's negative-point status (comparative).
On using Grain Seeds: optional extra grain+veg; if taken, each opponent gets grain from supply.
Reed each time another player renovates; plus one-time reed on play in 3p.
Using Cattle Market accumulation space: +1 cattle (must be accommodated).
Each time another renovates, optional exchange 2 wood -> grain+food+bonus point.
Place cattle on round spaces +5/+8, collected at start of those rounds.
When another uses Fencing, optional buy sheep/boar/cattle for food.
Once 5+ rooms, at any time once ever add a room at no cost; deferrable grant of build-room, free.
On Farmland/Cultivation, pay 1 food to plow an additional field.
Place field tiles on rounds +4/+7/+10; at start of those rounds optionally plow the field for 1 food.
Immediate 2 wood; after each new room built, build/play an improvement for 1 wood less.
On play take a begging marker (a negative goods effect). At start of each harvest may play an occupation free — grants a card-play action; EXOTIC for the begging-marker penalty.
Before the final harvest (round 14), may sweep all building resources from the entire board — reads goods across all board spaces.

cards.new_category: BUY-VP: pay a resource to gain a bonus point (VP purchased during play, not an end-game scoring rule).
cards.note: After using Grain Utilization, may pay 1 food to buy a bonus point placed on the space; the food goes onto the action space for the next visitor.
Each time you gain a new field tile (plow sub-action outcome), auto-gain 1 wood + 1 food.

cards.new_category: BUY-VP: discard goods to gain a bonus point.
cards.note: In returning-home phase, optional use but if used must choose 3 food OR 1 bonus point, discarding all clay. Optional-to-use so arguably F-TRIG but the pick between food/point is a mandatory choice once invoked.
Each harvest may convert exactly 1 stone into 3 food — a deferrable conversion capped once per harvest.
Each time you renovate, place 1 food on each of next 6 round spaces, collected at round starts.
On play place 2 grain + 2 veg on card (a per-card goods stack); buy them at any time paying food, consumed over the game.
During breeding, per newborn gained may sow crops in exactly 1 field — grants a sow sub-action per newborn.
On play place 6 wood on card; immediately after each card play (incl. this one) gain 1 wood from the card's stack.
Before using a Build Fences/Rooms space, choose to get 2 wood OR exchange up to 2 wood for reed; clarification excludes card-granted actions. Optional overall but forks into two options.
On play, place 1 clay on each remaining round space 6-14, collected at those round starts.
Continuous cost replacement: renovate-to-clay costs 1 clay+1 reed; each clay room costs 3 clay+2 reed.
At scoring may pay 1 food per unused farmyard space to avoid its -1 penalty — a scoring-time rule paid with food (convertible).
On-play +1 grain; each time you use Grain Seeds space, +1 grain (before-action, auto).
Taking >=4 of same resource from accumulation space grants +1 that resource + 1 food; keyed on the take action.
Before each later occupation play, +2 food (explicitly before paying cost).
Using round-space 1-8 places food on card; at 3 food move to supply. Round-number band = board position; goods stacked on card.
On-play +2 clay; at-any-time clay->stone conversion (2clay->1stone, 3clay->2stone).
Building 2nd/3rd/4th stable grants a cattle/boar/sheep; needs running stable count; animals must be accommodated.
When you first live in clay/stone house, may pay 1 grain+2 food (food cost) to make card a room holding one person; one-time on standing condition.
Continuous cost reductions: wood rooms -2 wood, 3rd/4th stable -1 wood, 13th-15th fences free.
On-play may build 1 stable for 1 wood; passive: one unfenced stable holds up to 3 animals (raises existing slot capacity).
Using Farmland/Cultivation with first person placed in a round, may plow 1 extra field for 1 food; needs to know it's the first placement of the round.
Immediately before each harvest, with house room, may take Family Growth for 3 food.
Using Forest/Clay Pit: may place 1 stone on the other space and if so place another person; usable twice a turn.
At any time, take Build Stables action without placing a person; each stable costs 1 wood + 1 food.
On-play build Basketmaker's Workshop major for reduced/fixed cost 1 stone + 1 reed (grants+prices the major build).
After a Major/Minor or Minor Improvement action, pay 1 food to take it a second time — after-timing hook granting a repeat action; food cost.
When people return home from both Grain Seeds and Vegetable Seeds spaces: gain 2 food and may play an occupation free. Fires at returning-home; grants a free occupation play (no cost, no placement).
On play +1 wood; each harvest field phase may exchange 1 wood+1 food for a bonus point (repeatable conversion into VP during harvest).
At start of scoring, place food in empty fields (treated as planted) for bonus points per threshold. Places crops on fields as part of a scoring rule.

If played by round 11, 4 bonus pts if more rooms than people. Rooms-vs-people is own-state (E-SCORE); the round-11 play restriction is a computable prereq. Unclear whether the round condition needs any state beyond play-time check — leaning plain scoring rule.
Once per round, exchange 2 grain for a bonus point + food scaled by unfenced-stable count. Deferrable capped conversion yielding VP.
After using a wood accumulation space, pay 4 wood onto it to get 2 stone/1 clay/1 reed/1 grain. After-timing on named-space use; pays wood for goods.
At start of each round, make exactly one listed exchange (some yield animals needing accommodation). Start-of-round optional single-exchange.
Once each harvest, buy 1 wood/1 reed/1 grain for 2 food. Once-per-harvest purchase paid in food.
Before start of each round, if food >= round number, get 1 food. Start-of-round auto gain conditional on a plain count read.
After using a clay/stone accumulation space, pay stone/clay onto the space to get grain/veg. After-timing named-space hook; resource conversion.
Each time you build a major, place 1 reed on the card (kept pile); at any time move it to supply or exchange for a Sow action. Goods stack on card + deferrable grant-sub/gain.
On play +1 wood; each subsequent occupation played, +1 wood per food paid as its cost. Fires on card-play, keyed to the food-cost provenance of that play.
Each time you use an action space orthogonally adjacent on the board to one occupied by your own worker, get 1 wood. Board-adjacency + which spaces your workers occupy.
On play place 8 clay on card; each time you obtain grain, take 1 clay from the card's stack.
During breeding phase of each of first 6 harvests, optionally buy 2 stone for a food price that varies by harvest number (needs a per-card counter).

Before each work phase, optionally place a person on a building-resource accumulation space not in your supply — an extra out-of-turn placement with a supply-dependent restriction; unclear whether best modeled as start-of-work extra placement vs at-will.
On play pay food = complete rounds left, then take Family Growth even without room.
Changes wood-room cost to 1 reed + a wood amount that decreases by round band.
Use the Urgent Wish for Children space even if occupied by another player's first person only.
Each time you use Grove/Hollow, may place 2 reed on the other space and then immediately place another person; repeatable.
Each time you use Grain Seeds while a Lessons space is occupied, may play an occupation for 1 food (a granted play-occupation action, conditional on named space's occupancy).
On renovating to stone, place up to 1 wood in each room; each such wood scores 1 bonus point (must remember which wood is placed).
1 bonus pt per stone-wood pair in supply; those resources cannot also score elsewhere (mutual exclusion).
1 bonus pt per farmyard space (incl. rooms) holding at least 1 cattle — a coverage/count score.
On play, wood based on rounds left; at scoring, each player with a highest-capacity pasture gets 3 pts (comparative across players).
Each time any player plays or builds a cooking improvement, you get 1 wood + 1 food (fires on own and opponent actions).
On Day Laborer, get choice of 1 sheep or 1 grain; may instead buy 1 boar for 1 food or 1 cattle for 2 food (animals need accommodation).
On play +1 reed +1 food; at-any-time convert 1 reed -> 2 food.
On play +1 grain; each time you get a Minor Improvement action you may take Bake Bread instead (substitute action).
Any player uses Sheep Market -> you get 1 grain (fires on own and opponent use).

On play buy 2 stone for 1 food; then one-per-round buy 1 stone for 2 food (ATWILL+CAP-ROUND, buy=convert food->stone). Unclear if 'buy' is a scheduled/at-will option — treated as at-will purchase, no future-space placement; E-SCHED likely not apt but flagged uncertainty.
After any player uses unoccupied Grove/Forest while the other is occupied -> +1 reed; depends on occupancy of the paired named space.
On play place a resource pair on each improvement (per-card goods stacks); each time another player renovates you may move one pair to supply (gain goods).
Each time you use an accumulation space, place 1 clay on card; must convert 4 clay->1 boar held on card (card holds animals).
Each time another player renovates to stone, if you live in clay house, pay 2 food to build a clay room at no additional cost (granted sub-action with food cost + cost override).

On your turn, pay 1 grain to use the same space the right-neighbor just used (uses an already-used/occupied space; needs to know which space they placed on). Unclear whether best as L-OCCUPY vs L-EXT; treated as extended eligibility to reuse a used space.
After another player uses Grain Utilization -> you get a Sow action (granted sub-action, optional).
Another player uses Traveling Players -> pay them 1 food to play an occupation free (pay opponent + granted play-occupation action).

Each time you use one of the two twin accumulation spaces with exactly equal goods, may buy 1 bonus point for 1 food. Buying VP via food; comparison of the two spaces' good counts. Unclear whether 'bonus point' purchase is E-SCORE (it's a bought VP, not a scoring rule) — flagged.
On play +1 grain and (at start of this returning-home phase) food = number of occupied action-space cards; counts occupied round-space cards on the board.
On play add 1 cattle to Cattle Market (places animal on a market space), then get X grain + X food per cattle on Cattle Market.
Before start of each round, conditional food gain based on out-resourcing opponents in >=2 types; round-start hook, auto.
Before each person placement, if >=8 wood on accumulation spaces, get 1 food; reads board accumulation state.
Start of each round, gain difference of food between two named accumulation spaces; auto, references named spaces.
Before using the most-recently-revealed action space card, get 1 grain; depends on board reveal order/position.
On play, tiered veg gain based on count of unplanted field tiles; plain conditional goods.
Card is an action space for all; opp using it gives them 3 wood + must give you 1; you get 4 wood.
Each time any player takes 5/6/7/8+ goods from accumulation space, tiered resource gain; needs the amount taken (provenance).
Each time any player uses Pig Market, you get 1 sheep; animals must be accommodated.
On play pay 1 food per remaining harvest to get 1 cattle + 1 wild boar; food cost + animals.
Each use of Day Laborer, optionally get 3 different animals instead of 2 food, then pay 1 food per remaining harvest; substitute action outcome + animals + food cost.
Once you have 6 occupations, card provides room for one person; standing-count latch creating a person holder.
Keep 1 sheep on each occupation card in front of you; each card becomes an animal holder.
Immediate 1 grain; passively may replace up to 2 building resources per type with 1 grain each on builds/renovate.
Each time you build >=1 fence, may also build a stable free of wood; granted sub-action with zeroed wood cost.
After each person action (from r5), optional Family Growth if room; costs 2 negative points (a scoring rule). Growth needs room in house.
Once per work phase, after a person action, pay 1 sheep + 2 food to return another placed person home to reuse; needs to know which worker went where.
On Build Rooms with 2nd placed person, mandatorily return the first placed person home (unless on Meeting Place); needs to know which space that worker is on.
On play, immediately build a major improvement; may replace up to 1 building resource of each type with 1 food each (cost substitution).
After final harvest, 1 bonus point per person for which you pay 1 clay (optional per-person). Play-round condition (r4 or before) is a plain prerequisite.
During scoring, 1 bonus pt per category scoring max 4; a plain threshold/coverage score, not an optimization. No firing.
At start of each returning-home phase, optionally return a built stable to supply for wood+grain+food and a Minor Improvement action.
On using Fishing/Reed Bank, optionally pay 1 food to place another person on this card, gaining choice of 3 stone OR grain+veg (mandatory choice within the optional action). Card is an action space.
Each time you use a clay accumulation space +1 food; a stone accumulation space +1 grain. Automatic goods gain keyed on named space types.
At any time, pay 1 grain to place 4 food on the round-space 2 rounds ahead, collected at that round's start. Scheduling onto a future round space.
On play +1 wood; each time after using an action space with a Sow action, +2 food (automatic, after-sow).

Continuous rule altering how much other named accumulation spaces refill based on Fishing's food level. Unclear whether modeled as passive accumulation-rate change or a per-refill hook (S-SOR); treated as PASSIVE altering accumulation amounts.
Each use of Major Improvement space also +1 grain (auto) and afterward may take a Sow sub-action (optional grant).
Per grain harvested from a field, optionally buy 1 veg for 3 food (2 food if the last grain from a field). Fires during field harvest phase; needs to track last-grain state.
Card is a personal Wood accumulation space (holds/stacks wood); on the reveal of a Quarry card you must discard all wood from it. Reveal-hook keyed on the newly revealed card's identity.
On play +2 wood; passive cost reduction: pay 1 food to reduce each improvement by up to 2 wood (food-for-resource cost substitution).
On play +2 clay; once per round can buy 2 clay for 2 food (deferrable conversion).
After building each wood/clay room, get 2 clay/2 stone; hook on build-room sub-action.
At will place a good on the card to get 1 stone; goods placed must all be DIFFERENT types, so card tracks which goods already on it (a stack of distinct goods).
Each time you use a wood/clay accumulation space, may buy exactly 1 building resource for food; hook on using a specific named space, optional.
Pile of goods on card (ST-STACK, set up on play); each time you harvest a field tile, may take top good. Hook is per-field-tile in the field phase.
Card is a personal action space providing Day Laborer / Building Rooms / Major Improvement, but only if each other player has more rooms than you (comparative gate on both players' rooms).
After another player builds rooms, you may give them 1 food to build exactly 1 room yourself (paying its cost); opponent-triggered granted build-room sub-action plus a food transfer to them.

Each harvest with >=5 wood, may discard down to 5 wood to take a Build Stables or Build Wooden Rooms action. Timing within harvest not precisely stated (tagged S-HSTART); discard-down mechanic; grants a whole build action. Unclear exactly when in harvest it fires.
At end of each work phase, if you did not use Meeting Place, may take a Build Rooms action without placing a person; needs to know whether Meeting Place was used this round.
On play +1 stone; passively lets you build bottom-row major improvements when taking a Minor Improvement action (extends what that action can build).
In the feeding phase of each harvest, may turn 1 wood + 1 grain into 1 bonus point and 2 food; conversion yielding a bonus point plus food.

cards.new_category: SKIP-PLACEMENT: forces the player to skip their next person placement this round (deferrable to a later turn).
cards.note: Each time Fishing is used (by any player) you get 1 bonus point but must skip placing your next person that round. Fires on your own use of Fishing (A-OWN assumed); the skip-a-placement penalty fits no existing code. Actor scope (own vs any) slightly ambiguous.
On-play wood based on rounds-left (plain state read); end-game bonus for most veg in fields, compared across players.
On-play wood; end-game bonus for most fenced stables, comparative across players.
After using a Lessons space, may buy up to 2 different goods with food (a food-rate conversion/purchase); clarification confirms it's deferrable but keyed to the after-space event.
Each time you use a 1-animal accumulation space, may take from general supply instead (leaving space), plus 3 food + 1 grain; provides an animal to accommodate.
When another player uses Meeting Place, both they and you get 1 food (before the action); when you use it, you get 1 food. Fires on opp and own action; grants goods to opponent too.
Each time you take >=4 building resources or >=4 animals from an accumulation space, also get 1 food; conditional on the amount taken (payload of the space).
At end of each work phase, if you occupy Clay Pit or Reed Bank while the other is unoccupied, get 1 veg; needs board-occupancy check keyed to end of phase.
Each time you use an accumulation space giving >=3 of one type (except wood), get +1 wood; depends on the space's payload.
After using Fishing or one of the three orthogonally adjacent action-board spaces, get +1 reed; depends on action-board adjacency.
On play, reed scaling with number of major improvements (plain state read).
Each time you use Day Laborer, place food/food/boar on next 3 round spaces, collected at start of those rounds; schedules a good and an animal.
Continuous: hold 2 sheep on the border between each pair of orthogonally adjacent rooms; new holder created based on room adjacency (shape).
Each time another player uses a Quarry, you choose 1 food OR build a stable without paying wood (must choose one). Grants a stable sub-action (piece cost), fires on opponent action.
Taking Family Growth with your second person placed in a round returns your first-placed person home (to reuse); manipulates an already-placed worker, needs placement order log.
After using Traveling Players, may place another person on any space even if occupied -> extra placement + use occupied space.
At start of each harvest, conditional cumulative bonus points based on grain fields; scored via harvest-start firing.
Renovate-to-stone costs 2 stone less (cost mod); bonus points per OTHER player in stone house (comparative).
Each harvest may convert 1 wood -> 1 food + 1 grain; card-hosted optional conversion each harvest.
Places 3 grain+3 food on card (stack); each Resource Market use draws 1 grain+1 food from card. Named space; treated non-geom.
When opponent gains 5th person, one-shot +8 food (fires on opp gaining a person, not retroactive); comparative scoring if only you have 5 people.
Anytime turn 1 reed -> 3 food, but any opponent may buy the reed for 2 food to prevent it -> opponent-interaction transfer.
When another player uses their FIRST placed person for Family Growth, you get 1 grain; needs to know it was their first placement.
Each time any player renovates, may buy 1 veg for 3/2/1 food depending on improvements built alongside; needs the renovate event's follow-on build count.
On play +2 stone; each time another player renovates to stone or builds a stone room, you get 1 stone.
After using an animal accumulation space, if no animal in house, also get 1 sheep; must accommodate.
After using an animal accumulation space, if you occupy the space directly above/below it, also get 1 boar -> board adjacency.
Each time you build >=2 stables on one turn, also get 1 cattle.
On play +1 wood; after each non-harvest round, may breed exactly one animal type outside a breeding phase -> off-harvest breeding.
On turning boar/cattle to food, optionally bank that food on card; while banked food equals #rooms, card holds 1 person (new person-holder slot). Conditional capacity on a stored stack.
At-will place wood on card irretrievably; card holds 2x animals per wood on it (new animal holder scaling with stored stack).
End of work phase rounds 7&9: optional Renovation without placing, 1 building resource cheaper.
Once in stone house: each round start, optionally pay 2 or 3 wood to build up to 3 or 4 fences. Stone-house standing condition gates the ongoing hook.
After playing 2nd/3rd/5th/7th occupation, optionally build 1 stable free; needs occupation-count log.
Each time you get 2+ newborns, optionally pay 1 food to plow a field.
Can build Joinery via Minor Improvement action; if Joinery used during harvest, pay 1 food to plow 1 field.
Once this game, if 2-room house surrounded by 4 field tiles, use Wish for Children even without room. Geometry adjacency of fields around house; family growth without room.
On first turn each round, if no unused farmyard spaces, place an extra person from supply.
On play, take a Renovation action then optionally a Build Fences action, both at usual cost.
On play may build a baking improvement at cost; each time another player uses Grain Seeds space, optionally take a Bake Bread action.
Play at start of round-1 work phase without placing a person; no other effect (just counts as occupation). Special no-place play timing.
If 3rd occupation, immediately play another occupation (1 food) and/or a minor improvement at cost.
If 1st occupation, immediately gain 1 bonus point per current improvement (one-time immediate VP gain on play, not an end-game scoring rule).
At end of each harvest (after feeding), if stone house, gain a bonus point — fires per-harvest conditional on standing house state; accrues bonus VP.
At start of each work phase, if you hold all 6 good types, gain a bonus point; fires per-round conditional on supply state.
On play: bonus points per complete stage left; then bars future occupation play. Stage-count read of board progress; play-restriction on occupations.
Start of each round, optionally pay food = #people to buy 1 good of choice; food cost may need conversion.
Pile clay/wood/grain on card; each time you obtain the good matching the top item, may move it off and gain a wild boar. Ordered stack consumed over time.
If played by round 4, schedule 3 veg on round-11 space, collected at that round's start.

On play and each time before using the most-recently-revealed action space card, gain 1 building resource + 1 food. Keyed to the most recent revealed card (board recency); must track which space that is.
On play, optionally convert up to #people building resources to food at fixed rates.
In each harvest field phase, gain food scaled by field-count thresholds; plain count read.
Each time you build fences, place 1 food on each remaining round space up to number of fences just built; collected at those rounds' starts. Needs count of fences built this action.
Each harvest, may exchange 1 reed->2 food (once per harvest); plus lets you build Basketmaker's Workshop for altered cost via a Minor Impr. action (extends legality + cost formula).
At start of each harvest may irretrievably place 1 wood on card; each feeding phase gain 1 food per wood on card. Accumulating pile drives per-feeding income.
While you have no minor improvements played, gain 1 food each round start and 1 wood each harvest start; conditional per-round/per-harvest income (two hooks, both auto).
When harvesting a grain field, may leave the grain on the field and take 1 grain from general supply instead; alters the field-harvest yield/behavior.
Each Family Growth action -> +1 veg; keyed on the growth sub-action, mandatory gain.
Building nth stable gives grain/veg; needs running count of stables built.
After using Grain/Veg Seeds space, may take a Bake/Sow sub-action (optional grant).
On play +1 food; after using an animal accumulation space, wood scaled by which person number placed (needs count of persons).
At start of each harvest, if >=1 grain field, +2 wood.
Each time you get food from an action space, +1 wood; keyed on obtaining food (space-sourced).
If played by round 4, schedule 4 wood + 4 clay on round-9 space, collected at that round's start.
Place alternating wood/clay on next 6 round spaces, collected at start of each.
Each time you use Grain/Veg Seeds space, also +2/+3 clay.
Each time you play/build an improvement, get 1 wood OR 1 clay (mandatory choice) after paying cost; fires on both play-minor and build-major.
A fixed pile of resources on the card, spent top-first toward build-room/play-build-improvement/renovate costs.
On play +2 wood +2 stone; end-game -1 pt per wood/stone in supply; removes the ability to discard wood/stone (a currency/option restriction).
On play +1 building resource; after all people placed this round, may place an additional person (extra placement).
Latches on when living in a stone house; thereafter each round-start gives a mandatory choice of 2 wood/2 clay/1 reed/1 stone.
On-play, if you'd max 4pts in 3 categories, grant a free room build (build-room sub-action at no cost); references scoring categories to gate.
After building a major improvement, optionally pay 1 food to plow 1 field.
While a person is on Day Laborer, may use occupied non-accumulating round 1-9 spaces.
Each use of a Wish for Children space, play 1 extra improvement at cost less 1 resource of choice.
After placing last person of round on Traveling Players, play an occupation for 1 food cost; needs to know it was the last placement.
Once per harvest, exchange grain+veg pair for 6 food; scoring option to trade pairs for bonus points.
Placing 2/3+ newborns during breeding gives 1/2 bonus points; scored per breeding event but counted as end-game bonus points.
Each harvest optionally place 1 crop or newborn on card irretrievably (stack of goods); once-game exchange for bonus points. Fires on harvested crop/newborn.
On play, immediate wood by rounds-left; comparative scoring (most vegetables between players).
On play, immediate wood plus a Build Fences action by rounds-left; comparative scoring (most pastures).
Each use of Reed Bank also get grain; each use of Grain Seeds also get reed.
On play (round 11 or before), get animals of chosen type equal to number already owned; must accommodate.
On play, place food on a chosen future round space (distance-based), collected at start of that round.
Only next round: each use of a building-resource accumulation space also yields 1 food per resource taken; needs a flag/window for 'next round'.

Each time you use Major Improvement or Vegetable Seeds space, also get 1 veg or a Major/Minor Improvement action respectively — after-space hook, mandatory-choice per space (the granted action is a whole Maj/Min Improvement action).
In each harvest's feeding phase, optionally exchange up to 2 goods wood->grain and/or grain->stone (chainable to wood->stone).
From round 3, at end of each work phase where all clay accumulation spaces unoccupied, get 1 stone+1 food; needs to read occupancy of clay spaces at work-phase end.
Each time any player plays/builds a card enabling resource->food, you get 1 corresponding building resource + 1 reed; needs which resource that card converts (provenance).
If played round 7 or before, choose clay or reed and double your current supply of it; play-timing restriction is a computable condition.
Once this game, immediately after playing/building an improvement, optionally get its printed cost from supply — after-play hook, once-per-game, needs the card's cost (provenance) and a used flag.
At start of each harvest, based on fenced-stable count (1/2/3+), get 1 sheep/boar/cattle; animals must be accommodated.

cards.new_category: STABLE-ON-SPACE: place a marker (stable) on named action spaces that builds for free when another player uses that space
cards.note: Pre-place up to 1 stable on 4 named spaces; when an opponent uses that space, build the stable free — grants a free build-stable sub-action triggered by opponent's space use; needs per-space marker state.
At start of last harvest, optionally take up to 2 of each opponent's unbuilt fences and build them free (>15 allowed) — grants free fence building at a specific harvest, pulls pieces from opponents.
May use an occupied space that provides stone + another building resource; stone rooms cost 2 stone less each — continuous legality + cost change.
Once this game, if you have all animal types, use a Wish for Children (family growth) space even without room — growth without room; needs used flag. E-GROWTH note: even without room.
At start of each round, optionally place 1 food on Traveling Players to play a minor improvement by paying its cost — a food-cost self-placement enabling a play-minor action without a worker.

Each harvest, optionally exchange exactly 1 stone for 1 bonus point + 1 food; stone->VP+food conversion once per harvest. Note: 'each harvest' timing ambiguous but resolves as a harvest-scoped option.

Once in stone house (latch enables), get 2 food each time any player renovates + score 1 pt per wood/clay house — the renovate hook (after-renovate, own+opp) is gated by the stone-house standing condition; scoring is comparative over both players' house materials.

Round-4-or-before condition gates immediate goods+boars (accommodate); family-growth delay until round 11 is a play-restriction on the growth action computable from state — no clean code, but core is onplay goods/animals.
Fires on another player playing/building an improvement with printed clay cost -> you get food+clay; provenance of clay cost read.
On first-occupation play, food = count of other occupations in play (any player) capped at 7; plain count read at play.
At end of each work phase, food per stone accumulation space with stone; counts board stone spaces.
Continuous feeding reduction (1 less food per person) + rewritten people scoring rule (2 pts not 3).
When another player uses Fishing: they get +1 food, you get 1 veg; named space hook, affects opponent's gain too.
Card is your own action space giving 3 grain (no person placement); also extends Minor-Improvement action to allow building Stone Oven major.
Each round start, optionally move/discard 1 food to/from card to get a building resource of a type you lack; food stored on card.
Each time after you play a later occupation, get 1 building resource of choice.
Each time you plow >=1 field, get 1 sheep per tile plowed; needs count of tiles just plowed (provenance).
On play +1 boar, and each time you build a major improvement +1 boar.
Each use of Traveling Players/Fishing, optionally move 1 food from that space to the other to get 1 cattle.
On play each player (incl you) chooses 2 sheep or 2 food; you also get 1 cattle; opponent gains from your card.
Start of each round, animal per unoccupied room by material (wood->sheep/clay->boar/stone->cattle); depends on rooms holding people (E-CAPNEW-ish) but core is scheduled animals per round.
